description: This chart plots density (unit: kg/m³) on the y-axis, comparing three datasets for alloying elements relevant to ferritic/low alloy steels: apparent density of ferrite (calculated as the inverse of regression coefficients from Model II, represented by horizontal bars with 95% probability error bars), pure elemental density (square markers), and average literature-reported apparent density (triangle markers) for elements including Cr, Cu, Fe, Mn, Mo, Ni, S, Si, V. The underlying regression model for ferrite density has an R² of 0.994, maximum deviation of 4.2 kg/m³, and average deviation of 1.4 kg/m³. Close agreement between regression results and reference values is found for Cr, Cu, Fe, Mn, and S, with minor deviation for Mo, and larger deviations for Ni, Si, V. The results confirm that Cu and Mo increase ferrite density, while C, Cr, Mn, Ni, S, Si, and V reduce ferrite density, supporting the reliability of the developed density prediction model for ferritic steels.
